subject: Human Resources
title: Platform Workers and Labor Rights
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Platform Workers and Labor Rights.

hook.text: A delivery driver works 12-hour shifts, earns less than minimum wage after expenses, and has no sick leave.

buildup.text: Platform workers—Uber, DoorDash, Instacart—are algorithmically managed but legally independent, creating a protection gap.

discovery.text: Algorithmic management controls work assignment, pricing, and deactivation—exerting employer-level control without obligations.

twist.text: Drivers can be 'deactivated' (fired) by an algorithm with no appeal, no explanation, and no unemployment benefits.

climax.text: Labor movements are evolving—portable benefits, minimum earning guarantees, and algorithmic transparency are emerging solutions.

punchline.text: You can't call someone independent while an algorithm controls their every move.

quiz.question: What is algorithmic management in platform work?
options: When algorithms control work assignment, pricing, and deactivation
When workers manage their own schedules freely
When AI helps workers earn more
